title: ranunculus glacialis subsp. camissonis
intro: Ranunculus glacialis subsp. camissonis, also known as the glacier buttercup, is a plant species native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. The plant contains glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus glacialis subsp. camissonis include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tincture or infused in oil, and its medicinal properties can also be preserved through freeze-drying and other herbal preparations.
